content.content: Me: I am not sure what a Statement of Purpose is.
Facilyn: A Statement of Purpose captures the positive change your organisation creates. It's the "why" behind everything you do.
Facilyn: For example, a hospital's purpose isn't "To make money from sick people" — instead, it can be described like this: "To heal and restore health."
Facilyn: A factory's purpose isn't just "To make things" — it might be "To create tools that improve people's lives" or "To transform raw materials into useful products."
Facilyn: Which purpose fits your organisation best?

Knowledge:

Complexity

Complexity refers to self-organising systems that in contrast to machines we do not produce, but manage.

Whole Under Management

The Whole Under Management is the “unit” you are managing and making decisions for. It is defined by three things:
• Decision makers
• Resource base
• Money

Decision makers

Anyone who makes day-to-day decisions, carries them out, or has veto power over them. Being inclusive prevents hidden constraints later.

Resource base

Everything you rely on to support your quality of life and plans. It includes physical resources (land, buildings, equipment) and human resources (people, partners, customers, neighbours, communities).

Holistic context

Your Holistic Context is your shared “north star” for decision-making. It describes:
• The quality of life you want
• The future resource base needed to sustain it

Holistic context for organisations

For organisations, the Holistic Context also includes a statement of purpose for the organisation.
The seven filter questions:

Filter questions

They are the seven context checks that help you test any action against your holistic context, so you avoid solutions that look good today but create problems tomorrow.

When an action fails a check

It does not automatically mean “never do it”. It means you have learned where the risk is. You can modify the action, choose a different action, or proceed knowing what you must monitor and control.

Cause and effect

Does this action address the root cause of the problem?

Weak link

Social: Could this action, due to prevailing attitudes or beliefs, create a weak link between us and those whose support we need?

Biological: Does this action address the weakest link in the life cycle of this organism?

Financial: Does this action strengthen the weakest link in the chain of production?

Marginal reaction

Which action provides the greatest return toward the goal for each additional unit of time or money invested?

Gross profit analysis

Which enterprises contribute the most to covering the overheads of the business?

Energy/money source and use

Is the energy or money to be used in this action derived from the most appropriate source in terms of our holistic context?

Will the way in which the energy or money is used be in line with our holistic context?

Sustainability

If we take this action, will it lead toward or away from the future resource base described in our holistic context?

Gut feel

How do we feel about this action now?

Will it lead to the quality of life we desire?

Will it adversely affect the lives of others?

Why gut feel is last

Because it uses the picture you built from the other checks. It asks how you feel about the action in terms of your values, relationships, and consequences for others.

4_teahouse.how_mng_complexity.title: So, how do you manage complexity?

content.content: Me: So, how do you manage complexity?
Facilyn clears a small space on the wooden table. She moves the cups aside.
Facilyn: You manage it by refusing to lose sight of the whole you’re affecting.
Facilyn: And by filtering every significant decision against a context large enough to hold that whole.
Her hand remains resting on the cleared space between you.
Facilyn: The framework we created helps you do that.
Facilyn: If you want, I’ll walk you through it.
You enter the teahouse.
The noise of wind folds away as the door closes behind you.
Inside, the light is amber and low. Steam rises in slow spirals from clay pots. Cups click softly against saucers.
A thin thread of citrus and jasmine hangs in the air.
At a table near the window, a hand lifts in greeting.
It’s Facilyn.
Facilyn: You found it.
She looks at you and smiles.
Facilyn: I wasn’t entirely sure you would.
